name: Fête de l'Escalade
description: Commemoration of Geneva's victory over the Duke of Savoy's attack in 1602. Includes costumed parades, chocolate marmite breaking, Course de l'Escalade run, and children singing door-to-door.
notes: Largest historical festival in Geneva. Marmite tradition dates from 1880s.

name: Course de l'Escalade
description: Annual running race through Geneva's Old Town, linked to the Escalade celebrations. Multiple categories for all ages including costumed 'Marmite' race.
notes: One of Geneva's largest annual sporting events.

name: Fêtes de Genève
description: Major summer festival along the lakefront with concerts, food stalls, funfair, and one of Europe's largest fireworks displays over the Rade.
notes: Attracts over 2 million visitors over 10 days.

name: Promotions / Fêtes des Écoles
description: End-of-year school celebration marking students' passage to next grade. Tradition dating from 16th century with parades and festivities.
notes: One of Geneva's oldest continuous traditions.

name: La Revue genevoise
description: Annual satirical theatre show mocking local politics and human foibles. Running since 1892, a deeply Genevan cultural institution.
notes: Considered a living tradition of Geneva.

name: Marronnier de la Treille
description: Since 1818, the sautier (secretary of parliament) officially announces spring when the first leaf appears on the chestnut tree of Promenade de la Treille. Oldest phenological record in Europe.
notes: Continuous record since 1818; indicator of climate change.

name: L'Esprit de Genève
description: Set of humanist values (justice, independence, tolerance) associated with Geneva's history from Calvin to the Red Cross. Manifested through international organizations and cultural identity.
notes: Geneva's intangible cultural identity.

name: Tradition horlogère genevoise (La Fabrique)
description: Geneva's centuries-old watchmaking tradition. The 'Fabrique' refers to the distributed artisanal watchmaking system, with the Poinçon de Genève quality hallmark.
notes: Geneva hallmark since 1886. UNESCO-listed Swiss watchmaking tradition.

name: Fête nationale suisse (1er août) - Geneva
description: Swiss National Day celebrations in Geneva with speeches, fireworks over the harbour, lantern processions and folk songs.
notes: Geneva's lakeside setting makes for spectacular fireworks.

name: Bains des Pâquis traditions
description: Year-round communal bathing and social gathering at this iconic public bath on Lake Geneva. Fondue evenings in winter, swimming in summer. A beloved social institution.
notes: Iconic Geneva social institution, saved from demolition by popular vote in 1988.

name: Fête nationale - Versoix
description: Traditional 1st August celebration with lantern procession, bonfire, local food stalls, and fireworks on the lake.
notes: Small-town lakeside celebration.

name: Jardins en Fête - Château de Coppet
description: Annual garden festival in the grounds of the historic Château de Coppet, with garden competitions, plant sales, and demonstrations.
notes: Château historically associated with Madame de Staël's literary salon.

name: Giron des Musiques - Coppet
description: Traditional brass band festival bringing together local fanfares and music societies for parades and competitions.
notes: Part of the broader Swiss Giron tradition of music society gatherings.

name: Paléo Festival Nyon
description: Switzerland's largest open-air music festival. 6 days, 250,000+ visitors, 250+ concerts on multiple stages. Founded in 1976 as the Nyon Folk Festival.
notes: One of the largest open-air festivals in Europe.

name: Visions du Réel
description: International documentary film festival held annually in Nyon. One of the most important documentary film events in the world.
notes: Internationally renowned documentary film festival.

name: Les Hivernales de Nyon
description: Winter music festival in Nyon with concerts and cultural events during the cold months.
notes: Complements summer festival scene.

name: Roman heritage - Colonia Iulia Equestris
description: Nyon was founded as a Roman colony by Julius Caesar. Roman museum and annual events celebrate this heritage.
notes: Roman columns still visible in town.

name: Caribana Festival
description: Diverse musical programme over 4 days in Crans-près-Nyon on the shores of Lake Geneva.
notes: Intimate lakeside festival.

name: Fête nationale - Rolle
description: Traditional lakeside 1st August celebrations with bal populaire, cortège, and fireworks over the lake with the Île de La Harpe as backdrop.
notes: Picturesque setting with island.

name: Fête de la Tulipe
description: Annual tulip festival in the Parc de l'Indépendance behind Morges Castle, with 150,000 tulips in 350 varieties. Free admission, running since the 1970s.
notes: One of the largest free tulip displays outside the Netherlands.

name: Festival des Dahlias
description: Annual dahlia festival in the Parc de l'Indépendance, Morges, complementing the spring tulip festival.
notes: Extends the flower tradition through summer.

name: Les Mouettes de Morges
description: Regional section of the cantonal costume association (ACCV) combining folk dance, traditional Vaudois costumes, and songs in annual performances.
notes: Part of broader Vaudois folk dance tradition.

name: Fête nationale - Morges
description: Large 1st August celebration with cortège, concerts, DJ, food stalls, children's activities, dances, open-air cinema, and lakeside fireworks.
notes: One of the more festive lakeside celebrations.

name: Marché de Morges
description: Traditional weekly market in the old town of Morges, a centuries-old commercial tradition.
notes: Continuous market tradition.

name: Voiles latines de Lutry
description: Gathering of traditional Latin-rigged sailing boats on Lake Geneva, preserving historic lake navigation techniques.
notes: Preserves traditional lake sailing heritage.

name: Festival de la Cité
description: Free open-air performing arts festival in Lausanne's historic city centre (Cité). Theatre, dance, music, circus arts in medieval streets.
notes: Major free cultural event in the Cité quarter.

name: Lausanne Lumières
description: Festival of lights transforming Lausanne's landmarks with artistic light installations during the holiday season.
notes: Brings light to the darkest months.

name: Bô Noël
description: Lausanne's Christmas market with artisan stalls, local food, and holiday atmosphere. Final edition in 2025 under this format.
notes: Major festive gathering point.

name: Le guet de la Cathédrale
description: Since 1405, a night watchman calls out the hours from the belfry of Lausanne Cathedral from 10pm to 2am, every night. One of the last such traditions in Europe.
notes: Over 600 years continuous. Last night watchman tradition in Switzerland.

name: Prix de Lausanne
description: Prestigious international ballet competition for young dancers (15-18), held annually since 1973. Launching pad for world-class ballet careers.
notes: One of the most prestigious ballet competitions in the world.

name: Comptoir Suisse (historical)
description: Historically Switzerland's largest consumer fair, held in Lausanne from 1920 to 2019. A major Vaudois tradition for decades.
notes: Major commercial tradition, now ended but culturally significant.

name: Fête nationale - Lausanne
description: 1st August celebration at Ouchy with official ceremony, national anthem, concerts, Billie Bird, fireworks on the lake.
notes: Major lakeside celebration.

name: CGN Belle Époque steamers
description: Fleet of historic paddle steamers (Belle Époque boats) on Lake Geneva, some dating from the 19th century. Annual Naval Parade in May.
notes: 8 historic steamers, some over 100 years old.

name: Bol d'Or Mirabaud
description: Largest inland lake regatta in Europe, from Geneva to Villeneuve-Montreux and back. Over 500 boats. Founded 1939.
notes: Iconic Lake Geneva sailing event.

name: Fête des Vendanges de Lutry
description: Largest harvest festival in Canton de Vaud. 3 days of celebrations with wine tasting in caves, children's costumed parade (900+ children), concerts, market, regatta, and campagnard buffet.
notes: Over 30,000 visitors. Benefits go to children's holiday colony.

name: Lavaux vineyard terraces
description: UNESCO World Heritage vineyard terraces dating from the 12th century when Cistercian monks created them. Living agricultural landscape with Chasselas wine production.
notes: Exceptional cultural landscape linking Lausanne to Vevey.

name: Épesses Nouveau en Fête
description: Annual celebration of the new vintage in the Lavaux wine village of Epesses, with wine tasting and local food.
notes: Village-scale celebration of new Chasselas.

name: Marché des vins de Chardonne
description: Annual wine market in the Lavaux village of Chardonne, showcasing local Chasselas and other wines.
notes: Celebrating Lavaux wines.

name: Pâtés de la Dame (Lavaux)
description: Traditional charitable food distribution in Lavaux villages. A centuries-old social tradition.
notes: Ancient charitable tradition.

name: Championnat du monde des tracassets
description: World championship of tracassets - peculiar Vaudois vehicles (cross between tractor and old motorcycle) used in steep Lavaux vineyards.
notes: Uniquely Lavaux tradition combining agriculture and fun.

name: Régate des vieux bateaux
description: Regatta of old/historic boats on Lake Geneva organized from La Tour-de-Peilz.
notes: Preserves historic boat heritage.

name: Pain des veuves
description: Traditional distribution of bread to widows in La Tour-de-Peilz. An ancient charitable tradition.
notes: Centuries-old social welfare tradition.

name: Fête des Vignerons
description: UNESCO-listed once-per-generation festival celebrating winegrowers, organized by the Confrérie des Vignerons since 1797. Massive outdoor spectacle with 5000+ performers, 20,000-seat arena. Last held 2019.
notes: First Swiss tradition on UNESCO intangible heritage list. Next edition ~2039.

name: Foire de la Saint-Martin
description: Over 550-year-old autumn fair in Vevey (established by Duke of Savoy in 1470). Animal parade, terroir products, spit-roasted beef, papet vaudois, procession with farm animals.
notes: Bridge between mountain/rural and urban cultures.

name: Marchés folkloriques de Vevey
description: Traditional folklore markets in Vevey featuring local crafts, costumes, food, and cultural demonstrations.
notes: Showcasing regional folk traditions.

name: Confrérie du Guillon
description: Traditional wine brotherhood that holds elaborate feasts (ressats) at Château de Chillon, celebrating Vaud wines with ritual, song and gastronomy.
notes: Named after the guillon (wooden tap) used on wine barrels.

name: Foire de Brent
description: Traditional fair in the Vevey district village of Brent, with local products and community gathering.
notes: Local village tradition.

name: Montreux Jazz Festival
description: Iconic international music festival founded in 1967 by Claude Nobs. Two weeks of concerts on Lake Geneva shoreline. Second largest jazz festival in the world.
notes: World-famous, defines Montreux's cultural identity.

name: Montreux Noël / Christmas Market
description: One of Switzerland's finest Christmas markets with 150+ illuminated chalets along the lakefront, Père Noël volant (flying Santa), and Maison du Père Noël at Rochers-de-Naye.
notes: Spectacular lakeside Christmas atmosphere.

name: Septembre Musical
description: Classical music festival in Montreux, part of the rich musical tradition of the Swiss Riviera.
notes: Part of the classical music heritage.

name: Foire des Planches
description: Traditional fair in the Planches quarter of Montreux, with local products and community spirit.
notes: Named after the historic Planches neighbourhood.

name: Freddie Mercury tradition
description: Cult of Freddie Mercury in Montreux, centered on his lakeside statue (erected 1996). Queen recorded multiple albums at Mountain Studios. Annual fan gatherings.
notes: Mercury lived last years in Montreux; his statue is a pilgrimage site.

name: Château de Chillon
description: Most visited historic monument in Switzerland. Medieval castle on lake island near Montreux with continuous cultural programming, Christmas events, and the Guillon wine brotherhood feasts.
notes: Switzerland's most visited castle. Inspiration for Lord Byron's poem.

name: Noël au Château de Chillon
description: Christmas festivities at Château de Chillon with seasonal decorations, activities, and medieval atmosphere.
notes: Medieval Christmas atmosphere.

name: Fête nationale - Villeneuve
description: 1st August celebration at the eastern end of the City101 corridor with traditional lakeside festivities.
notes: Eastern terminus of City101.

name: Vendanges (wine harvest) tradition
description: The annual grape harvest across the entire Geneva-Villeneuve lakefront, from La Côte through Lavaux to Chablais. Community participation, wine pressing, celebrations.
notes: Defines the rhythm of lakeside communities.

name: Costume vaudois tradition
description: Tradition of Vaudois folk costumes and dances, maintained by the ACCV with regional sections across the corridor (Nyon, Morges, Lausanne, Montreux, etc.).
notes: Includes the iconic 'chapeau à borne' hat.

name: Cuisine vaudoise
description: Traditional Vaudois cuisine: fondue au fromage, papet vaudois (leek and sausage), filets de perche, malakoffs, Vacherin Mont d'Or, tarte au vin, gâteau du Vully.
notes: Defines regional food identity.

name: CGN steamboat crossings
description: Historic CGN (Compagnie Générale de Navigation) paddle steamer tradition on Lake Geneva. Belle Époque fleet, New Year's Eve cruise, Naval Parade.
notes: Historic fleet connects all lakeside communities.

name: Fête nationale du 1er août (corridor-wide)
description: Every lakeside town celebrates Swiss National Day with speeches, lantern processions, bonfires, folk songs, and fireworks over the lake.
notes: Unifying national tradition with local character.

name: Foire d'automne de Rolle
description: Traditional autumn fair in the small lakeside town of Rolle.
notes: Local market tradition.

name: Marché de Noël de Montreux
description: One of Switzerland's biggest and most atmospheric Christmas markets along Montreux lakefront.
notes: Over 150 decorated chalets.

name: Saint-Martin de Peissy (Geneva)
description: Annual celebration of end of grape harvest in the Geneva wine village of Peissy on the Saturday closest to November 11.
notes: Geneva's wine harvest tradition.

name: Tournée saucisses (Aigle/Ollon)
description: Traditional New Year's sausage distribution/procession in the Chablais region (Ollon, Aigle), near the eastern end of the corridor.
notes: New Year's food tradition.

name: Bénichon / Bénichon-like traditions
description: Autumn harvest festival with traditional meals (bénichon menu with cream, cuchaule bread, meringues). More associated with Fribourg but celebrated in Vaud border areas.
notes: Bridges Vaud-Fribourg cultural boundary.

name: Cor des Alpes tradition
description: Alphorn playing tradition maintained by groups like L'Echo du Bois-Rond covering Geneva to Lausanne region. Performances at festivals and national celebrations.
notes: Iconic Swiss instrument, actively practiced along corridor.

name: Marchés folkloriques d'Echallens
description: Weekly folklore markets every Thursday in July in Echallens (hinterland of Lausanne). Legacy of the Fête du Blé et du Pain tradition.
notes: Directly linked to the Fête du Blé et du Pain.

name: Giron des Musiques de Lavaux et du Gros-de-Vaud
description: Annual rotating gathering of fanfare societies from Lavaux and Gros-de-Vaud (Pully, Cully, Belmont-sur-Lausanne, Grandvaux, Echallens, etc.). Includes band competitions, soloist contests, parade, and communal festivities. Rotates host village each year.
notes: Includes ~14 societies from the corridor. 2022 in Pully, 2026 in Mézières.

name: Giron des Musiques de la Riviera
description: Rotating annual gathering of fanfare societies from the Riviera district (Vevey, Montreux, La Tour-de-Peilz area). Band competitions, parades, and festivities.
notes: Part of the SCMV network of 105 Vaudois music societies.

name: Giron des Musiques de La Côte
description: Rotating annual gathering of fanfare societies from the La Côte district (Nyon, Rolle, Morges area). Band competitions, parades, communal festivities.
notes: Covers the western stretch of the City101 corridor.

name: Giron du Centre - Jeunesses campagnardes
description: Annual summer gathering of rural youth societies (jeunesses campagnardes) from the Centre region covering Lausanne hinterland. Athletic competitions (athletics, cross-country, tug-of-war, wrestling, football, volleyball), communal meals, and festivities. Rotating host.
frequency: Annual (rotating host, or Cantonale every 5 years)
notes: Part of FVJC (9,000 members, 200 sections). Tradition created to combat rural exodus.

name: Giron du Pied-du-Jura - Jeunesses campagnardes
description: Annual summer gathering of rural youth societies from the Pied-du-Jura region (Morges-Nyon hinterland). Six sporting disciplines, communal festivities, intergenerational gathering.
notes: 76th Giron FJDN was held at Marchissy (near Nyon) in 2025.

name: Fête cantonale des Jeunesses campagnardes (FVJC)
description: Every 5 years, all 4 regional Girons merge into a single cantonal festival lasting 3 weeks. Replaces annual girons. All sporting disciplines, official ceremonies, anthem singing, flag ceremonies.
notes: Unites ~9,000 members from 200 sections across Vaud.

name: Fête fédérale de gymnastique (Lausanne 2025)
description: Switzerland's largest sports festival, held in Lausanne in 2025. 70,000 athletes, 23,000 youth. Competitions in artistic gymnastics, athletics, team gymnastics, national games. Tradition since 1832.
notes: Held in Lausanne in 1909 and 2025. Major community tradition.

name: Fêtes cantonales de gymnastique vaudoise
description: Every 6 years, all Vaudois gymnastics societies gather for cantonal competitions. District-level girons held in intermediate years. Tradition since 1851.
notes: Major sporting and social tradition.

name: Tir cantonal / Sociétés de tir
description: Annual and periodic shooting competitions organized by local shooting societies (sociétés de tir). Deep Swiss tradition linked to military service and civic identity. Multiple societies along the corridor.
notes: Constitutional right to bear arms linked to militia tradition.

name: Sociétés de chant / Chorales
description: Network of local choral societies (sociétés de chant) across the corridor. Annual girons/gatherings, cantonal festivals. Deep roots in Vaudois communal life.
notes: Multiple societies in every lakeside town.

name: Vevey Spring Classic
description: Annual spring classical music festival in Vevey on the shores of Lake Geneva. International soloists perform Mozart, Brahms, and other major composers.
urban_zone: Vevey - Salle del Castillo / Musée Jenisch
notes: 5th edition in 2026. Growing cultural event.

name: Foire de Brent (Montreux)
description: Traditional annual fair in the village of Brent above Montreux. Local products, community gathering.
notes: Mountain-lakeside connection.

name: Marché de Noël de Vevey
description: Christmas market on Place du Marché in Vevey with 20 stands, ice rink, gourmet animations at Alimentarium, and Vaud terroir products.
notes: Showcases Vaudois terroir products.

name: Fête du Bois (various locations)
description: Periodic celebrations of woodworking and forestry traditions in Vaudois villages. Connected to the broader Swiss timber and alpine economy heritage.
notes: Part of rural craft heritage.

name: Abbaye de Tir / Sociétés de tir locales
description: Local shooting society tradition (Abbaye/Société de tir) present in most corridor towns. Annual competitions (tirs), communal meals, civic ceremonies. Some dating to medieval period.
notes: Intimately linked to Swiss civic identity and militia system.

name: Bande dessinée genevoise
description: Geneva has a strong tradition of comics and illustrated posters (affiche illustrée), recognized as a living tradition. Includes festivals, publishers, and artist communities.
notes: Rodolphe Töpffer, considered inventor of the comic strip, was Genevan.

name: Tradition botanique genevoise
description: Geneva's botanical tradition dating from the 18th century, centered on the Conservatoire et Jardin botaniques. Annual observation of the Marronnier de la Treille (already listed) is part of this broader tradition.
notes: World-renowned herbaria and botanical research.

name: 20km de Lausanne
description: One of Switzerland's top 3 road races, held annually in spring. Massive popular participation, a defining Lausanne sporting tradition.
notes: Over 25,000 runners. Major community event.

name: Athletissima
description: International athletics meeting in Lausanne, part of the Diamond League. One of the world's top athletics events, held at Stade de la Pontaise.
notes: World-class athletics in the Olympic Capital.

name: Tradition olympique de Lausanne
description: Lausanne is the Olympic Capital, hosting the IOC since 1915, the Olympic Museum, and multiple international sports federations. This institutional identity shapes the city's cultural life.
notes: Defines Lausanne's international identity.

name: Cully Jazz Festival
description: Major spring jazz festival held annually in the wine village of Cully (Lavaux). 140+ concerts over 9 days, performances in wine cellars and open-air stages. Founded 1984, 43rd edition in 2026.
notes: Performances in wine cellars integrate terroir and music.

name: Illumination de la Cathédrale de Lausanne
description: Traditional illumination of Lausanne Cathedral, a living tradition of the canton. The cathedral's nighttime lighting and special illumination events are part of the city's cultural identity.
notes: Combined with the guet tradition.

name: Coupe de Noël de Genève
description: World's largest winter swimming event, held annually since 1934. 4,400+ participants swim 100m in the Rade at 5-10°C water. Inscribed to Swiss national living traditions in 2023. Originally inspired by the Parisian Coupe de Noël.
notes: World's largest cold-water swimming event. 87th edition in 2025.

name: Nage en hiver / Baignade hivernale lémanique
description: Growing tradition of winter swimming in Lake Geneva, practiced year-round at Bains des Pâquis and Plage des Eaux-Vives. Moon swims, lighthouse circuits. Recognized as Swiss living tradition in 2023.
notes: Part of broader Léman culture.

name: Navigation à voiles latines sur le Léman
description: Latin-rigged sailing on Lake Geneva, dating to 13th century. Barques à voiles latines are traditional cargo boats. Inscribed Vaud cantonal heritage 2021, Swiss national 2023. UNESCO candidacy submitted 2025 (decision expected late 2026).
notes: Multinational UNESCO candidacy with Croatia, France, Greece, Spain.

name: Régates lémaniques
description: Network of 150+ annual regattas organized by 33 clubs (ACVL, 4500 members). Includes Bol d'Or, Semaines du soir, Régate des Vieux Bateaux de La Tour-de-Peilz. Recognized as Swiss living tradition 2023.
notes: 33 clubs, 4500 members, tradition since 1872.

name: Fête de Mai de Begnins
description: Ancestral spring celebration in Begnins (above Nyon-Gland). Children's parade, music, games. Revived in 2022 after 7 years' absence. Part of cantonal living traditions inventory.
notes: Important for the thin Rolle-Gland corridor zone.

name: Foire des Planches (Montreux)
description: Traditional fair at Les Planches quarter in Montreux. Listed in cantonal living traditions alongside other historic fairs.
notes: Distinct from Foire de Brent.

name: La Verrée vaudoise
description: Traditional communal wine-drinking gathering in Vaud. A verrée marks official occasions, inaugurations, civic events. The shared glass of local wine is a fundamental social ritual.
notes: Fundamental social ritual across all Vaudois communities.

name: Contes et légendes lémaniques
description: Tradition of local legends and tales particular to the Léman shores: lake monsters, pirates of the Léman, castle ghosts, vineyard spirits. Active storyteller communities maintain the tradition.
notes: Includes Léman pirates legend, Château de Chillon stories.

name: Patois vaudois
description: The Vaudois dialect (Franco-Provençal), once the everyday language of the region. Now threatened but maintained through associations, courses, and cultural events. Cantonal living tradition.
notes: Endangered but actively maintained by associations.

name: Pêche professionnelle traditionnelle du Léman
description: Traditional professional fishing on Lake Geneva using historic methods. Filets de perche (perch fillets) are the iconic local dish. Fishermen's cooperatives maintain the tradition.
notes: Source of iconic filets de perche dish.

name: Cueillette des narcisses (Riviera)
description: Traditional narcissus picking on the Riviera hills above Montreux. Spring tradition linked to the Fête des Narcisses (historically). Cantonal living tradition.
notes: Narcissus fields above Montreux.

name: Société internationale de sauvetage du Léman (SISL)
description: The Léman rescue society, a tradition of volunteer lake safety and rescue since 1885. Stations all along the lake shore. Cantonal living tradition.
notes: Volunteer tradition along entire corridor.

name: Canots en bois du Léman
description: Traditional wooden boat (canot) building and maintenance on Lake Geneva. Distinct craft tradition linked to the lake's maritime heritage. Cantonal living tradition.
notes: Includes restoration of historic boats.

name: Pratiques mortuaires vaudoises
description: Distinct Vaudois funerary customs and practices, including the communal repast (repas funéraire), the cortège, and particular cemetery traditions. Cantonal living tradition.
notes: Distinct from French or Germanic practices.

name: Paysannes vaudoises
description: Network of Vaudois rural women's associations organizing educational, social, and cultural activities in villages. A key community institution particularly in the corridor's smaller towns.
notes: Important social infrastructure in villages.

name: Milices vaudoises
description: The Vaudois militia tradition, inherited from the cantonal defense system. Now expressed through shooting societies, Abbayes, and civic ceremonies.
notes: Links to shooting societies and Abbayes.

name: Carillons et cloches
description: The tradition of church bells and carillons marking time and events along the corridor. Each town has its particular bell-ringing patterns for services, festivals, funerals, and civic occasions.
notes: Soundscape tradition defining the corridor's acoustic identity.

name: Salée au sucre et raisinée (vin cuit)
description: Vaudois salée au sucre (sweet cake) and raisinée/vin cuit (cooked grape or fruit syrup). Traditional autumnal production in the wine regions.
notes: Linked to grape harvest traditions.

name: Guérisseurs / Rebouteux
description: Traditional healers (guérisseurs, rebouteux) still active in the Vaud countryside. Secret formulas passed down through families. Cantonal living tradition.
notes: Still active, discreetly, in rural areas.

name: Messager boiteux
description: The Messager boiteux de Berne et Vevey, an annual almanac published since 1708, transmitting weather lore, gardening advice, local news, and folk wisdom. Published in Vevey.
notes: The oldest continuously published almanac in the Romandie.

name: Lois coutumières vaudoises
description: Traditional customary laws and community rules (droits d'usage, communs) still governing aspects of village life, especially in agricultural and viticultural communities.
notes: Living legal tradition.

name: Giron du Pied-du-Jura à Saint-Prex (2025)
description: The 2025 Giron du Pied-du-Jura was hosted at Saint-Prex — the first time this major giron came to the lakeside. Major multi-day event with athletics, wrestling, tug-of-war, football, communal festivities.
notes: First time at Saint-Prex — directly on the thin corridor zone.

name: Fête nationale Saint-Prex
description: Saint-Prex 1er août celebration with cortège led by Fanfare de la Jeunesse, speeches, traditional fire, and fireworks over the lake. Active local associative life.
notes: Fanfare de la Jeunesse leads cortège through the medieval bourg.

name: Secrets de Saint-Prex / Genius Loci
description: Promenade through Saint-Prex's medieval bourg with Genius Loci medallions and QR codes revealing hidden histories of each site. Active heritage interpretation.
notes: Digital-physical heritage trail through medieval town.

name: Béjart Ballet Lausanne
description: Founded by Maurice Béjart in 1987, the company is a defining cultural institution of Lausanne. Listed as a cantonal living tradition alongside Prix de Lausanne.
notes: Major international dance institution.

name: Compagnie Philippe Saire / Linga
description: Lausanne and Pully-based contemporary dance companies listed as cantonal living traditions. Part of Lausanne's identity as a dance capital.
notes: Compagnie Linga based in Pully — directly on corridor.

name: Fête du Bois de Lausanne
description: Traditional celebration of wood and forestry in Lausanne, linked to the Jorat forest. Cantonal living tradition.
notes: Linked to broader Swiss forestry traditions.

name: Régate des Vieux Bateaux de La Tour-de-Peilz
description: Annual regatta of historic/vintage sailing boats at La Tour-de-Peilz. Part of the cantonal living traditions inventory for Lake Geneva regattas.
notes: Vintage boats from multiple eras.

name: Donation Bonnard (La Chiésaz)
description: Traditional charitable food donation at La Chiésaz (near Blonay/Saint-Légier, above Vevey). Listed alongside Pain des veuves and Pâtés de la Dame in cantonal traditions.
notes: Third of the corridor's charitable food traditions.

name: Journée des communes vaudoises
description: Annual gathering of Vaudois municipalities, a civic tradition reinforcing cantonal identity and inter-communal solidarity.
notes: Civic tradition linking all corridor communes.
